benchmark_scope: A narrow support-readiness operator workflow that creates a case, evaluates grounded evidence, records a human decision, and exposes no automatic-send capability. It is not benchmarked as a replacement helpdesk or autonomous customer agent.

criteria.label: End-to-end operator flow
criteria.market_expectation: Current support workspaces make queue state, the selected case, its evidence, and the next operator decision inspectable in one coherent workflow.
criteria.gap: The declared local create/evaluate/review slice is complete; multi-channel helpdesk breadth is outside scope.

criteria.label: Trusted-source grounding
criteria.market_expectation: Market systems manage real knowledge sources and constrain answers to approved, audience-appropriate content rather than source identifiers alone.
criteria.gap: The allowlist, citations and confidence gates execute, but source content and audience permissions are recorded fixtures rather than retrieved, versioned knowledge.

criteria.label: Human handoff and safe failure
criteria.market_expectation: Sensitive, uncertain, frustrated, unavailable-provider, or otherwise unresolved cases must reach a visible human-owned state with a safe fallback.
criteria.gap: The declared workbench route is safe and send-incapable; it does not yet hand a live conversation to a real team/channel.

criteria.label: Reasoning, source review, and feedback loop
criteria.market_expectation: Reviewers can inspect why an answer was produced, see the sources used, rate quality, and feed corrections back into governed knowledge.
criteria.gap: The UI exposes route, reason, citations, confidence and review status, but it lacks reviewer identity, append-only feedback history, source rating and knowledge-correction workflow.

criteria.label: Pre-publish test and regression gate
criteria.market_expectation: Behavior changes are previewed against representative cases before reaching customers and are protected by repeatable regression checks.
criteria.gap: The recorded regression harness is strong for this slice; representative real-store cases and a publish/rollback lifecycle remain absent.

criteria.label: Authentication, roles, and tenant isolation
criteria.market_expectation: A market system protects customer and administrative surfaces with authenticated identities, role-scoped permissions, and tenant/data isolation.
criteria.gap: No authentication, authorization, reviewer identity, tenancy, retention policy, or untrusted-network deployment boundary exists.

criteria.label: Live channel, platform, and provider contracts
criteria.market_expectation: Operational products connect governed knowledge and authorized actions to real support channels and platform/provider contracts.
criteria.gap: There is no live Gorgias, Shopify, carrier, channel, model, retrieval or authorized action adapter.

criteria.label: Outcome analytics and quality monitoring
criteria.market_expectation: Operators can filter/drill into conversation outcomes and monitor resolution, escalation, intent, response-time and quality trends using defined metrics.
criteria.gap: Local counts, routes and latency exist, but there are no real conversations, outcome definitions, trends, drill-down, satisfaction, alerts or quality-calibration loop.

criteria.label: Audit, governance, and privacy
criteria.market_expectation: AI-assisted support retains inspectable response/source/action history while protecting customer data through explicit access, privacy and governance controls.
criteria.gap: Decision/citation state is inspectable and fixtures contain no customer data, but review history is mutable and identity, encryption, retention, deletion, consent and privacy operations are absent.

criteria.label: Operability, observability, and recovery
criteria.market_expectation: A market-quality service has health/readiness, safe failures, controlled deploy/migration behavior, monitoring, incident ownership, backup/recovery and rollback evidence.
criteria.gap: Health, migration ordering, locked builds and disposable PostgreSQL smoke pass; production logs, alerts, SLOs, backups, restore, rollback and incident operation do not.

criteria.label: Accessible responsive operator experience
criteria.market_expectation: The workflow remains understandable and operable across viewport, keyboard, focus, error and pointer-target conditions.
criteria.gap: Responsive, focus, semantic state and overflow evidence exists, but no complete WCAG 2.2 AA, assistive-technology, zoom, contrast or browser-matrix audit exists.

criteria.label: Buyer, user, and market validation
criteria.market_expectation: Quality is confirmed with representative users and live outcome evidence after controlled deployment, not inferred from authored fixtures or visual review.
criteria.gap: No buyer interview, representative operator usability run, live store/channel, customer outcome, funded work or collected cash exists.
